question: A 40 year male complaining of hot flushes each time he baths. Hb: 20%gm, Platelet: 89,000/mL, WBC: 30,000/mL, Investigation revealed JAK2 mutation. What is the most likely diagnosis? The options are: (A) PMF (B) CML (C) PCV (D) Essential thrombocytosis
Mandibular Molar is The options are: (A) Tritubercular teeth (B) Quadritubercular teeth (C) Haplodont (D) Triconodont
Coxsackie virus causes The options are: (A) infectious mononucleosis (B) Lymphoma (C) herpangina (D) herpes
Artery palpable at the anterior border of the masseter is : The options are: (A) Superficial temporal artery (B) Facial artery (C) Lingual artery (D) Maxillary artery
Ligamentum flavum consists of which fibres:- The options are: (A) Type-I collagen (B) Type-II collagen (C) Reticular (D) Elastic
Which of the following is a method of achieving slow separation? The options are: (A) Seperating rubber bands (B) Rubber Dam Sheet (C) Ligature wire (D) All of the following

All of the following are true for ZOE based sealer except? The options are: (A) Expands on setting & hence provide impervious seal (B) Absorb if extruded periapically (C) Antibacterial, so prevent entombing of Bacterial (D) Slow setting, so results in increased inflammation initially
Which of the following is not involved in blood clotting? The options are: (A) Calcium (B) Prothrombin (C) Fibrinogen (D) Fe+2

An adolescent school girl complaints of dropping objects from hands, it gets precipitated during morning and during exams. There is no history of loss of consciousness and her cousin sister has been diagnosed with epilepsy. EEG was done and was suggestive of epileptic spikes. What is the diagnosis? The options are: (A) Juvenile myoclonic epilepsy (B) Atypical absence (C) Choreo - athetoid epilepsy (D) Centrotemporal spikes

All of the following are disadvantages of composite restoration except: The options are: (A) Greater localized occlusal wear (B) Recurrent secondary caries (C) Technique sensitive (D) More time in replacement
About carey coombs murmur which is false – The options are: (A) Delayed diastole murmur (B) Seen in rheumatic fever (C) Can be associated with AR (D) Low pitched murmur

Use of lithium during pregnancy increases the risk of development of which of the following malformations in the baby? The options are: (A) Facial defects (B) Cardiac defects (C) Neural tube defects (D) Urogenital defects

Which of the following is not true regarding trigeminal nerve? The options are: (A) Roots of trigeminal nerve are attached to Pons (B) It has three main divisions (C) Trigeminal nerve supplies the dura mater of middle cranial fossa (D) The trigeminal ganglion contributes to the sensory root only
The trivalent influenza vaccine contains all of the following strains except: The options are: (A) H IN I (B) H3N2 (C) H2N 1 (D) Influenza B
The iron carbide in orthodontic wire is in the form of: The options are: (A) Martensite (B) Ferrite (C) Carbide (D) Austenite
The common site for necrotizing sialometaplasia The options are: (A) cheeks (B) dorsum of tongue (C) palate (D) gingival

A tooth can be made to appear shorter by The options are: (A) Positioning gingival height of contour gingivally (B) Positioning gingival height of contour incisally (C) Positioning mesial and distal line angles closer (D) Positioning developmental depressions further apart
Full mouth disinfection is done in: The options are: (A) 24 hours (B) 48 hours (C) 72 hours (D) 36 hours

The decreased phosphate levels seen in hyper parathyroidism is due to: The options are: (A) Decreased intestinal phosphate absorption (B) Increased calcium excretion (C) Decreased renal phosphate absorption (D) Increased loss of phosphate in urine

The least effect on the prognosis of a periodontal disease is The options are: (A) Suppuration from the pocket (B) Furcation involvement (C) Alveolar bone loss (D) Co-operation and motivation of the patient

During cobalt chromium crown reconstruction, temperature of soldering torch suddenly increases. In such case, which of the following property will change? The options are: (A) Yield strength decreases and percent elongation increases (B) Yield strength and percent elongation both increases (C) Yield strength increases and percent elongation decreases (D) Yield strength and percent elongation both decreases
Which of the following are characteristic feature of high copper amalgam alloy? The options are: (A) Low compressive strength (B) High marginal breakdown (C) Less marginal # (D) High creep

A 16-year-old female with normal pubic hair and breast development, presented with complaints of primary amenorrhea. Investigation shows normal karyotype. Most likely diagnosis is:AFMC 12; JIPMER 12; AIIMS 12, 13 The options are: (A) Mullerian agenesis (B) Turner syndrome (C) Testicular feminization (D) Kallmann syndrome
Pickling is done: The options are: (A) To remove oxide film from casting (B) Polish the casting (C) Improve the strength of casting (D) Avoid casting defects

Tumor cells in chronic lymphocytic leukemia or small lymphoblastic lymphoma (CLL/SLL) arisefrom which of the following? The options are: (A) Mature B cell (B) Naive B cell (C) Centrocytes of germinal center (D) Progenitor B-cell
Best method to neasure HbA1c? The options are: (A) lsoelectric focusing (B) Affinity chromatography (C) Ion exchange chromatography (D) Electrophoresis
In which of the following microvilli are not present? The options are: (A) Gallbladder (B) Duodenum (C) Collecting duct (D) Proximal convoluted tubule

The treatment of choice for primary grade V vesicoureteric reflux involving both kidneys in a 6 month old boy is – The options are: (A) Antibiotic prophylaxis (B) Ureteric reimplantation (C) Cystoscopy followed by subureteric injection of teflon (D) Bilateral ureterostomies.
The community periodontal index of treatment needs is recorded for: The options are: (A) Quadrants (B) Sextants (C) One arch (D) Whole dentition

A research was undeaken by a group of psychiatrists and obstetricians to assess postpaum depression in mothers giving bih to male versus female children according to the Edinburgh depression scale (EPDS). What test should be used to compare the outcomes? The options are: (A) Student's t-test (B) Paired t-test (C) Chi-square test (D) Pearson's correlation coefficient
Which of the following is least sensitive to radiation exposure The options are: (A) Bone (B) Hematopoietic tissue (C) Nerve (D) Muscle

Treatment resins are given for which type of edentulous 
ridges? The options are: (A) Abused ridges (B) Flabby ridges (C) Ridges with large tori on them (D) Ridges with bony resorption
Staging of GTN with lung metastasis. The options are: (A) Stage 1 (B) Stage 2 (C) Stage 3 (D) Stage 4

A 10 years old child has intrusion of permanent maxillary central incisor. The choice of treatment is The options are: (A) Put tooth in its place and splint it (B) Allow tooth to erupt on its own (spontaneous eruption) (C) Treat it orthodontically (D) Do nothing
Cells most commonly affected in glaucomatous optic atrophy? The options are: (A) Amacrine cells (B) Bipolar cells (C) Ganglion cells (D) Rods and cones
Post-auricular ecchymosis in cases of fracture of the base of the skull is called: The options are: (A) Battle's sign (B) Tinel's sign (C) Trousseau's sign (D) Nikolsky's sign
Auriculotemporal nerve encircles which artery The options are: (A) Superficial temporal artery (B) Superficial temporal vein (C) Middle meningeal artery (D) Deep temporal
Most commonly missing tooth is The options are: (A) Upper 3rd molar (B) Lower 3rd molar (C) Upper lateral incisor (D) Lower 2nd premolar

Acetazolamide is given to a patient of angle closure glaucoma. It is a non- competitive inhibitor of carbonic anhydrase enzyme. Which of the following should be the effect of this drug? The options are: (A) Decrease in Vmax (B) Decrease in Km (C) Decrease in both Km and Vmax (D) No change in Vmax
The most common gene defect in idiopathic steroid resistant nephrotic syndrome – The options are: (A) ACE (B) NPHS 2 (C) HOX 11 (D) PAX
A 10 year old boy presents with midline swelling arising from cerebellum the diagnosis is – The options are: (A) Astrocytoma (B) Glioblastoma multiforme (C) Ependymoma (D) Medulloblastoma
The main vessel involved in bleeding from Juvenile nasopharyngeal angiofibroma: The options are: (A) Facial aery (B) Ascending pharyngeal aery (C) Internal maxillary aery (D) Anterior ethmoidal aery
Radiation affects all three germ layers (ectoderm mesoderm and endoderm) in which of the following The options are: (A) Teratoid cyst (B) Dermoid cyst (C) Thyroglossal cyst (D) Branchial cyst

Which of these is not true about randomization in a clinical trial? The options are: (A) Reduces confounding (B) Decreases selection bias (C) Ensures comparability of two groups (D) Increases external validity of the trial
Best way to monitor perfusion of free flap: The options are: (A) Pulse oximeter (B) Laser doppler velocimetery (C) Prick test (D) Flourescin & dermo flourometer
For root fracture it is imperative to take X-ray at? The options are: (A) 45°,	90° (B) 90° (C) 90°,	110° (D) 45°,	90°, 110°
Sorting a patient of trauma of head injury on basis of need of care and availability of resources The options are: (A) Triage (B) Emergency care (C) Definitive care (D) Hospital care
what does not occur in 5'- 3'direction? The options are: (A) DNA repair (B) DNA replication (C) RNA editing (D) Transcription

When a person lies down : The options are: (A) There is immediate increase in venous return (B) Heart rate becomes stable at slightly more than normal (C) Cerebral blood flow increases & becomes stable at more than normal (D) Blood flow to the Apex of lung decreases

Which of the following statements regarding Kawasaki disease is true – The options are: (A) Associated with coronary artery aneurysm in up to 25% of untreated cases (B) It is the most common cause of vasculitis in children (C) IV immunoglobulins are recommended only if coronary artery is involved (D) Lymph node biopsy is used for diagnosis
